---
title: "plot evolutionary trajectory for multiple models"
author: "Andrea Chen"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: pdf_document
---

# parameter for plotting
```{r setup, include=FALSE}
wd_path <- normalizePath(dirname(rstudioapi::getActiveDocumentContext()$path))
setwd(wd_path)
base_plot_folder <- "../plots/11_17/psi_invasion/"
plot_n <- c(2, 8, 20, 50) # group sizes to plot for both models
plot_shift_sizes <- c(40,80,120) # shift sizes to plot for both models
regimes <- c("inv", "fit", "ext", "ave")  # "inv" = inverse variance model, "fit" = fittest individual model
plot_reps <- TRUE  # whether to plot individual replicate series 
hcl_color_palette <- "Dark 2" # color pattern for lines 
tick_seq <- 9970:10300 # ticks to plot
```

# load evolutionary trajectory data
```{r}
data_df <- readRDS("../r_data/psi_invasion/summary.rds")
```

```{r}
data_df <- summary_stat_df
```

```{r}
reg = "ave"          # choose from "inv", "fit", "ext", "ave"
n = 50               # choose from 2, 8, 20, 50
shift_size = 120     # choose from 40, 80, 120
plot_folder = paste0(base_plot_folder, "psi_mean/")
colname = "psi_mean"  # column to plot
if (!dir.exists(plot_folder)) {
  dir.create(plot_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

for (n in c(2,8,20,50)){
    for (reg in c("inv", "fit", "ext", "ave")){
        filt_A <- list(reg = reg, n = n, start_psi = 0.2)
        filt_B <- list(reg = reg, n = n, start_psi = 0.5)
        filt_C <- list(reg = reg, n = n, start_psi = 0.8)

        title <- paste0(
        if (reg == "inv") "Homogenous group" else
            if (reg == "fit") "copy the best" else
            if (reg == "ext") "copy the extreme" else
                if (reg == "ave") "copy the average",
        " model (n = ", n, ")"
        )

        png(paste0(plot_folder, "reg_", reg, "_n_", n, ".png"), width = 2200, height = 1800, res = 150)
        par(cex.axis = 1.6, cex.lab = 2.0, cex.main = 1.0, mar = c(4.5, 6, 3, 1))

        plot_lines_multi(
            df      = data_df,
            filters = list(filt_A, filt_B, filt_C),
            ycol    = colname,  # << choose the column to plot
            labels  = list(
                title = title,
                x = "Tick",
                y = "Value"      # or any y label you want
            ),
            show_rep = TRUE,       # faded per-rep lines? TRUE/FALSE
            cols = c(
                hcl.colors(3, hcl_color_palette, rev = TRUE)[1],
                hcl.colors(3, hcl_color_palette, rev = TRUE)[2],
                hcl.colors(3, hcl_color_palette, rev = TRUE)[3]
            ),
            ylim = c(0, 0.01),
            xlim = c(9970, 10300)
        )

        leg_expr <- as.expression(lapply(c(filt_A$start_psi, filt_B$start_psi, filt_C$start_psi),
                                        function(v) bquote(psi == .(v))))
        legend("topright", bty = "n", legend = leg_expr,
            col = c(
                hcl.colors(3, hcl_color_palette, rev = TRUE)[1],
                hcl.colors(3, hcl_color_palette, rev = TRUE)[2],
                hcl.colors(3, hcl_color_palette, rev = TRUE)[3]
            ),
            lwd = 3, cex = 2.2)
        dev.off()

    }
}
```

## count how many replicates have non-zero psi_mean
```{r}
count_nonzero_psi_mean <- function(df, gen, reg = NULL, start_psi = NULL, n = NULL, tick_col = "tick") {
  # basic checks
  if (!tick_col %in% names(df)) stop("Column '", tick_col, "' not found.")
  if (!"psi_mean" %in% names(df)) stop("Column 'psi_mean' not found.")

  d <- df

  # coerce
  d[[tick_col]] <- suppressWarnings(as.integer(d[[tick_col]]))
  d$psi_mean    <- suppressWarnings(as.numeric(d$psi_mean))

  # filter: chosen generation & finite psi_mean
  d <- d[d[[tick_col]] == gen & is.finite(d$psi_mean), , drop = FALSE]

  # optional filters (any of these can be NULL to skip)
  if (!is.null(reg)       && "reg"       %in% names(d)) d <- d[d$reg       %in% reg, , drop = FALSE]
  if (!is.null(start_psi) && "start_psi" %in% names(d)) d <- d[d$start_psi %in% start_psi, , drop = FALSE]
  if (!is.null(n)         && "n"         %in% names(d)) d <- d[d$n         %in% n, , drop = FALSE]

  # keep only rows with psi_mean != 0
  d <- d[d$psi_mean != 0 & d$tick>=10000, , drop = FALSE]

  # If all three identifiers provided (single combination), print single count
  if (!is.null(reg) && !is.null(start_psi) && !is.null(n)) {
    cat(sprintf("gen=%s, reg=%s, start_psi=%s, n=%s -> count(psi_mean != 0) = %d\n",
                as.character(gen), paste(reg, collapse="|"),
                paste(start_psi, collapse="|"), paste(n, collapse="|"),
                nrow(d)))
    return(invisible(d))
  }

  # Otherwise, print counts for all (reg, start_psi, n) combos present
  # (only among rows with psi_mean != 0 at this generation)
  if (!all(c("reg","start_psi","n") %in% names(d))) {
    stop("Data frame must contain columns: 'reg', 'start_psi', 'n'.")
  }

  out <- aggregate(
    list(count = rep(1L, nrow(d))),
    by = list(reg = d$reg, start_psi = d$start_psi, n = d$n),
    FUN = length
  )

  if (nrow(out) == 0) {
    cat(sprintf("No rows with psi_mean != 0 at gen=%s (after filters).\n", as.character(gen)))
  } else {
    # nicely print
    for (i in seq_len(nrow(out))) {
      cat(sprintf("gen=%s, reg=%s, start_psi=%s, n=%s -> count = %d\n",
                  as.character(gen),
                  as.character(out$reg[i]),
                  as.character(out$start_psi[i]),
                  as.character(out$n[i]),
                  out$count[i]))
    }
  }
  invisible(out)
}

```

```{r}
gen = 10300
for (reg in c("inv", "fit", "ext", "ave")){
    for (p in c(0.2,0.5,0.8)){
        for (n in c(2,8,20,50)){
            count_nonzero_psi_mean(data_df, gen = gen, reg = reg, start_psi = p, n = n)
        }
        cat("\n")
    }
}
```

```{r}
mean(data_df[data_df$tick >= 9950, ]$fitness_mean)
```
